Trustworthy multimodal AI for healthcare 面向医疗场景的可信多模态 AI
Zhihui Chen 陈致晖
Ph.D. Student in Artificial Intelligence, National University of Singapore
新加坡国立大学人工智能博士生

I build evidence-grounded AI systems for clinical and other high-stakes settings, spanning interpretable medical deepfake detection, medical image editing data infrastructure, specialized-domain LLM detection, and multimodal Agent post-training. 我构建面向临床和高风险场景的证据驱动 AI 系统，近期工作覆盖可解释医学深度伪造检测、医学图像编辑数据基础设施、专业领域大模型文本检测与多模态智能体后训练。

Trustworthy and Agentic LLM 可信与智能体大语言模型 Multimodal Intelligence in Healthcare 医疗领域多模态智能
TikTok Research Intern: Multimodal Agent Post-Training 字节跳动研究实习：多模态智能体后训练
MiniMax 10x Team Fellowship 10x Team 合作计划资助
View Publications 查看论文 View Showcase 查看项目展示 Google Scholar
zhihui.chen(at)u.nus.edu GitHub LinkedIn

I am a PhD student in Artificial Intelligence at the National University of Singapore, supervised by Prof. Mengling Feng. My work builds trustworthy multimodal and agentic AI systems for high-stakes healthcare, connecting publishable research artifacts with deployable evaluation and post-training workflows.

Academic Output: ACL 2026 & EMNLP 2025 Main; 3 open medical AI datasets with 100K+ HuggingFace downloads.

Academic Service: Reviewer for NeurIPS 2026, IJCAI 2026, IEEE Affective Computing & T-CE; ACL 2026 Main Session Chair.

Industry Connection: TikTok multimodal Agent post-training and MiniMax-supported healthcare AI safety evaluation.

我目前是新加坡国立大学人工智能博士生，导师为 冯梦凌教授。 我的研究聚焦高风险医疗场景中的可信多模态与 Agentic AI，将可发表的研究资产与可落地的评测、数据和 post-training 工作流连接起来。

学术成果：ACL 2026 与 EMNLP 2025 主会议；3 个开放医疗 AI 数据集，HuggingFace 累计 10 万+ 下载。

学术服务：NeurIPS 2026、IJCAI 2026、IEEE Affective Computing、IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics 审稿人；ACL 2026 主会议 Session Chair。

产业连接：字节跳动多模态智能体后训练与 MiniMax 支持的医疗 AI 安全评测。

Featured Work 代表项目
View showcase 查看项目展示
Interpretable medical deepfake detection 可解释医学深度伪造检测

MedForge MedForge

A data-and-model framework that detects medically plausible image forgeries through localized evidence, expert-aligned reasoning, and a Localize-then-Analyze detector. 通过 MedForge-90K 基准、专家对齐推理和先定位再分析检测器，对医学影像伪造进行定位、判别和解释。

ACL 2026 Main ACL 2026 主会议 19 lesion types 19 类病灶 10 deepfake models 10 种伪造模型
Project Page 项目页面 Paper 论文
Healthcare AI safety evaluation and clinically verifiable Agent workflows 医疗 AI 安全评测与临床可验证 Agent 工作流

MiniMax 10x Team Fellowship 10x Team 合作计划资助

A compute-supported project connecting long-context, multimodal, and Agent capabilities with healthcare safety evaluation, data flywheels, and verifiable decision paths. 将 MiniMax 长上下文、多模态与 Agent 能力连接到医疗 AI 安全评测、数据飞轮和可验证决策路径。

MiniMax 10x Team 10x Team USD 2,000 compute grant 1.5 万元算力资助 Healthcare AI Safety 医疗 AI 安全评测
Showcase 项目展示 MiniMax MiniMax
Zero-shot LLM detection in specialized domains 专业领域零样本文本检测

DivScore DivScore

A robust detector for medicine and law that uses normalized entropy scoring and domain knowledge distillation without retraining on new domains. 面向医疗与法律等高风险领域，在无需新领域训练数据的前提下，稳健检测大模型生成文本。

EMNLP 2025 Main EMNLP 2025 主会议 +14.4% AUROC +14.4% AUROC +64.0% recall +64.0% 召回
Project Page 项目页面 Code 代码
Research Value 研究价值
Clinical AI Safety 临床 AI 安全

Evidence-grounded medical AI evaluation 面向医疗 AI 的证据驱动评测

MedForge turns realistic medical image tampering into localized, inspectable reasoning; the MiniMax fellowship extends this safety lens toward clinically verifiable Agent workflows. MedForge 将真实医学影像篡改转化为可定位、可检查的推理任务；10x Team 合作计划资助将这条安全主线延伸到临床可验证 Agent 工作流。

ACL 2026 Main · MiniMax 10x Team Fellowship ACL 2026 主会议 · 10x Team 合作计划资助
Specialized-domain Governance 专业领域治理

Robust detection for high-stakes generated content 高风险生成内容的稳健检测

DivScore targets medicine and law, where zero-shot LLM detectors must remain reliable under domain shift without new-domain detection labels. DivScore 面向医疗与法律场景，让零样本大模型文本检测器在没有新领域检测标注的情况下仍能应对领域偏移。

EMNLP 2025 Main · +14.4% AUROC EMNLP 2025 主会议 · +14.4% AUROC
Open Medical Data Infrastructure 开放医疗数据基础设施

Reusable assets for multimodal medical AI 可复用的多模态医疗 AI 资产

Med-Banana contributes open, medically controlled editing data and failure logs for training, evaluation, and preference-alignment research. Med-Banana 提供开放、医学约束下的编辑数据与失败日志，服务训练、评测和偏好对齐研究。

50,635 successful edits · 37,822 failed attempts 50,635 次成功编辑 · 37,822 次失败尝试
Selected Papers & Open Research Assets 精选论文与开放研究资产 (view all ) （查看全部 ）
MedForge: Interpretable Medical Deepfake Detection via Forgery-aware Reasoning 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Qingyuan Lei, Bin Pu, Jian Zhang, Yuling Xu, Mengling Feng# (# corresponding author)

Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL) 2026 Main Conference" data-zh=" 主会议"> Main Conference

As generative models improve, medical deepfakes that implant or remove lesions while staying visually plausible pose growing risks to clinical safety and the integrity of medical evidence. Most prior work reduces detection to binary real-vs-fake scoring with little insight into where manipulation occurs or why. We present MedForge, an interpretable framework that introduces MedForge-90K—the first large-scale explainable medical deepfake dataset spanning CT, MRI, and X-ray, covering 19 lesion types with forgeries from 10 state-of-the-art deepfake models, each paired with expert-guided localization and clinical-grade explanations—and MedForge-Reasoner, a detector trained with a Localize-then-Analyze chain-of-thought paradigm and Forgery-aware GSPO reinforcement learning. MedForge-Reasoner achieves state-of-the-art detection while producing localized, verifiable medical rationales.

[Paper] [Project Page] [Model] [Dataset]

MedForge: Interpretable Medical Deepfake Detection via Forgery-aware Reasoning 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Qingyuan Lei, Bin Pu, Jian Zhang, Yuling Xu, Mengling Feng# (# corresponding author)

Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL) 2026 Main Conference" data-zh=" 主会议"> Main Conference

As generative models improve, medical deepfakes that implant or remove lesions while staying visually plausible pose growing risks to clinical safety and the integrity of medical evidence. Most prior work reduces detection to binary real-vs-fake scoring with little insight into where manipulation occurs or why. We present MedForge, an interpretable framework that introduces MedForge-90K—the first large-scale explainable medical deepfake dataset spanning CT, MRI, and X-ray, covering 19 lesion types with forgeries from 10 state-of-the-art deepfake models, each paired with expert-guided localization and clinical-grade explanations—and MedForge-Reasoner, a detector trained with a Localize-then-Analyze chain-of-thought paradigm and Forgery-aware GSPO reinforcement learning. MedForge-Reasoner achieves state-of-the-art detection while producing localized, verifiable medical rationales.

[Paper] [Project Page] [Model] [Dataset]

DivScore: Zero-Shot Detection of LLM-Generated Text in Specialized Domains 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Yucheng Huang, Yunxiao Zhu, Mengling Feng

Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing (EMNLP) 2025 Main Conference" data-zh=" 主会议"> Main Conference

Detecting LLM-generated text in specialized and high-stakes domains like medicine and law is crucial for combating misinformation and ensuring authenticity. We propose DivScore, a zero-shot detection framework using normalized entropy-based scoring and domain knowledge distillation to robustly identify LLM-generated text in specialized domains. Experiments show that DivScore consistently outperforms state-of-the-art detectors, with 14.4% higher AUROC and 64.0% higher recall at 0.1% false positive rate threshold.

[Paper] [Code] [Project Page]

DivScore: Zero-Shot Detection of LLM-Generated Text in Specialized Domains 🔗

Zhihui Chen, Kai He, Yucheng Huang, Yunxiao Zhu, Mengling Feng

Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing (EMNLP) 2025 Main Conference" data-zh=" 主会议"> Main Conference

Detecting LLM-generated text in specialized and high-stakes domains like medicine and law is crucial for combating misinformation and ensuring authenticity. We propose DivScore, a zero-shot detection framework using normalized entropy-based scoring and domain knowledge distillation to robustly identify LLM-generated text in specialized domains. Experiments show that DivScore consistently outperforms state-of-the-art detectors, with 14.4% higher AUROC and 64.0% higher recall at 0.1% false positive rate threshold.

[Paper] [Code] [Project Page]

Med-Banana-50K: A Large-Scale Cross-Modality Dataset for Medical Image Editing 🔗

Zhihui Chen, et al.

arXiv preprint 2025

Recent advances in multimodal large language models have enabled remarkable medical image editing capabilities. However, the research community's progress remains constrained by the absence of large-scale, high-quality, and openly accessible datasets built specifically for medical image editing with strict anatomical and clinical constraints. We introduce Med-Banana-50K, a comprehensive dataset for instruction-based medical image editing spanning chest X-ray, brain MRI, and fundus photography across 23 disease types. The public release includes 50,635 successful bidirectional edits and 37,822 failed attempts with full conversation logs, enabling evaluation, preference learning, and alignment research for medically grounded image editing systems.

[Paper] [Code] [Dataset] [Project Page]

Med-Banana-50K: A Large-Scale Cross-Modality Dataset for Medical Image Editing 🔗

Zhihui Chen, et al.

arXiv preprint 2025

Recent advances in multimodal large language models have enabled remarkable medical image editing capabilities. However, the research community's progress remains constrained by the absence of large-scale, high-quality, and openly accessible datasets built specifically for medical image editing with strict anatomical and clinical constraints. We introduce Med-Banana-50K, a comprehensive dataset for instruction-based medical image editing spanning chest X-ray, brain MRI, and fundus photography across 23 disease types. The public release includes 50,635 successful bidirectional edits and 37,822 failed attempts with full conversation logs, enabling evaluation, preference learning, and alignment research for medically grounded image editing systems.

[Paper] [Code] [Dataset] [Project Page]
